title: Platform release-asset name selection (pure, no network)
action: Create src/update/asset.rs (declared via `mod asset;` in src/update/mod.rs, pub) with a pure function `expected_asset_filename(os: &str, arch: &str) -> Option<String>` mirroring install.sh's target-triple mapping exactly (install.sh, the OS/arch case statement): (linux, x86_64) -> Some("herdr-go-x86_64-unknown-linux-musl.tar.gz"); (linux, aarch64) -> Some("herdr-go-aarch64-unknown-linux-musl.tar.gz"); (macos, aarch64) -> Some("herdr-go-aarch64-apple-darwin.tar.gz"); (windows, x86_64) -> Some("herdr-go-x86_64-pc-windows-msvc.zip"); every other (os, arch) combination -> None (no published asset, matching install.sh's own unsupported-combination errors -- e.g. macOS Intel, any other architecture). Also add a thin, deliberately-untested wrapper `expected_asset_filename_for_this_host() -> Option<String>` that calls the pure function with std::env::consts::OS and std::env::consts::ARCH. Take the (os, arch) strings as plain parameters to the pure function specifically so it stays unit-testable without needing to run on 4 different platforms.

truths: expected_asset_filename("macos", "x86_64") and any other unsupported (os, arch) pair return None

artifacts.substantive: exports expected_asset_filename and expected_asset_filename_for_this_host with unit tests covering all 4 supported combos plus at least one unsupported combo, no TODO stubs

prohibitions: No network calls in this cell

verify: test -f src/update/asset.rs && grep -q '#\[test\]' src/update/asset.rs && grep -q 'mod asset' src/update/mod.rs && cargo test --quiet update

trace.outcome: Added src/update/asset.rs with pure expected_asset_filename(os, arch) mirroring install.sh's target-triple mapping (D1) plus thin expected_asset_filename_for_this_host() wrapper; wired mod asset; into src/update/mod.rs
